title: Programmatic Python API
description:
As a developer integrating Plexus
I want to run optimizations programmatically from Python
So that I can automate score improvements in my applications

## Acceptance Criteria

- AlignmentOptimizer class with clean API
- Async execution support
- Callback support for iteration monitoring
- Works outside CLI context
- Returns structured results

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Programmatic Python API for Optimization
  As a developer
  I want to run score optimization from Python code
  So that I can integrate it into automated workflows

Scenario: Basic programmatic optimization
  Given I have a PlexusDashboardClient instance
  And I have an MCP server instance
  When I create an AlignmentOptimizer:
    | Parameter | Value |
    | client | <client> |
    | mcp_server | <mcp_server> |
  And I call optimizer.optimize(scorecard="customer-service", score="empathy")
  Then the optimization runs asynchronously
  And I receive a result dictionary with:
    | Field | Present |
    | success | Yes |
    | status | Yes |
    | baseline_evaluation_id | Yes |
    | final_evaluation_id | Yes |
    | iterations | Yes |
    | improvement | Yes |

Scenario: Custom optimization parameters
  Given an AlignmentOptimizer instance
  When I call optimize with:
    | Parameter | Value |
    | scorecard | "test-sc" |
    | score | "test-score" |
    | days | 60 |
    | max_iterations | 5 |
    | improvement_threshold | 0.03 |
  Then these parameters are passed to the procedure
  And the optimization respects all settings

Scenario: Iteration callbacks for monitoring
  Given an AlignmentOptimizer instance
  And an OptimizationMonitor instance
  When I call optimize with on_iteration=monitor.on_iteration
  Then the callback is invoked after each iteration
  And the callback receives:
    | Data | Present |
    | iteration_num | Yes |
    | iteration_data | Yes |
    | hypothesis | Yes |
    | metrics | Yes |
    | deltas | Yes |
  And I can print real-time progress

Scenario: Dry run from Python API
  Given an AlignmentOptimizer instance
  When I call optimize with dry_run=True
  Then no score versions are created
  But I still receive analysis results
  And the return structure is identical to normal runs
```
